title: dysoxylum fraserianum
intro: Dysoxylum fraserianum, also known as Fraser's dysoxylum or Fraser's wood, is a species of plant belonging to the family Meliaceae. It is a tree native to the subtropical regions of Asia, particularly in China and India, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and protection against certain diseases. Dysoxylum fraserianum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
